# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment for our batch processing jobs. The pods run data processing tasks and don't need to interact with the Kubernetes API. Use default service account settings for simplicity. Focus on getting the jobs running reliably.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1beta2  # use a specific version of your api (in this case, v1beta2)
kind: Deployment   # we're creating an object type -> "Deployments" for K8s. Note that there is no kind defined here as it defaults to 'deployment'. We should define our own custom resource in YAML or JSON format if needed later on..  You may also use other kinds depending upon your requirement
metadata:   # metadata section -> contains name and labels/annotations for the object. (Name is required, Labels are optional)
    name: batch-processing-jobs     # replace with a unique identifier of this resource within our K8s cluster e.g., my-job
  namespace: default                # where in your kubernetes clusters to create these objects -> 'default' by the way is typical, but you can also use any other namespaces if needed (like testing or production)  
spec:          # spec section containing details about how this resource should be created. In case of a Deployment it would contain references and sizes for replicas etc..  We define here only pods as no data processing is happening yet in the job but will soon...
    selector:               # these selectors help with determining which Pod to use or reuse if we have more than one matching. This could be a label, an expression ect.... (it'd depend on your particular scenario)  -> here it would just match our pods labeled 'app=batch-processing'.
      matchLabels:   # labels that uniquely identify the Pod(s). Here we only have app as one key with its value being batch processing. It will be useful when you are applying these to other resources or using them for different jobs/environments in your K8S cluster  -> here it would just include 'app=batch-processing' and match any pod running this specific job
        app: batch-processing    # replace with the appropriate label, value pair(s) depending on how you want to identify objects within a namespace (like if there are more than one). E.g., {'app': ‘myjob’} or all ones where we can uniquely differentiate between pod in this case
   template:           # information about what should be created for the Pod(s) when using our Deployment -> here it will include details such as Docker image to use, how many replicas of each object (pod), and whether they get restarted on failures. 
     metadata:          # add your pod's Metadata  – this includes labels which must be included if you have one or more containers running in the Pod -> e.g., label 'app':my-job, name will also auto generated by K8S when a new object is created on cluster 
         labels:           # add your pod's Labels  – these are arbitrary and can be anything (e.g.'version', environment') if you want -> e.g., 'environment': production-ready, version:'v1’ then it will auto generate by K8S when a new object is created on cluster
           app: batch-processing    # same as matchLabels in the selector   – we need this to find where these Pod(s) are being deployed. This matches with 'app' label set during Deployment creation above and can be different from how you define your K8S resources later down stream, like so {'environment': production-ready,'version': v1}
     spec:              # will contain details of what should happen for a single pod   – here it includes image to use (from Docker), number or type and ports exposed/mapped etc...    -> you can also add more properties like environment variables, volumes based on your needs. Note that this section would not be included if we're running only one Pod within the Deployment itself
       containers:         # details about what to create for each container in our pod (and Docker image) – here it includes 'batch-processing', Image as docker/imageName, port settings etc...    -> you can also add more properties like environment variables and volumes based on your needs. Note that this section would not be included if we're running only one container in our pod
           - name: batch-processing  # replace with appropriate value (e.g., 'data_processor')  – each Container within the Pod has a unique Name which is also used as part of its Identifier when interacting through K8s API later on downstream if needed to find it ect…
             image: docker/imageName    # replace with appropriate Docker Image (e.g., 'my-data_processor') – this will be included in Deployment definition itself and would reference the right container images at different environments   -> as much detail about each one, what's needed or not...
             ports:          # details of how to expose port(s) for our Pod/Container. If it was running on a specific service within your K8S cluster (like if you have set 'type': Service in the spec), then we would reference this at runtime with appropriate endpoints ect....    -> here will be only one as per current requirement and might need to get dynamically changed based upon different jobs or environments.
